_comment: Tier 1 tools: External libraries that AI-Hydro integrates with - Updated for Phase 1 CAMELS implementations

pygridmet.description: Access gridded meteorological data from GridMET dataset
pygridmet.purpose: Download basin-averaged climate forcing data including precipitation, temperature, PET, solar radiation, wind speed, and humidity

dates.description: Start and end dates as (YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DD)

variables.description: List of variable names (pr, tmmn, tmmx, srad, vs, sph, pet)

outputs.description: Daily climate data with spatial dimensions and time

pygridmet.usage_example:
import pygridmet as gridmet

ds = gridmet.get_bygeom(
    geometry=watershed_geom,
    dates=('2010-01-01', '2020-12-31'),
    variables=['pr', 'tmmn', 'tmmx', 'pet'],
    crs='EPSG:4326'
)
key_features: 1/24th degree (~4km) spatial resolution
Daily temporal resolution from 1979-present
CONUS coverage
Multiple climate variables available

py3dep.description: Python API for retrieving gridded elevation, slope, aspect, and topographic products from USGS National Map 3DEP (3D Elevation Program) web services and staged datasets with multi-resolution support.
py3dep.purpose: Automated downloading and processing of DEM, slope, aspect, contour, and hillshade products for hydrological, geospatial, and scientific analyses. Enables workflows for watershed modeling, terrain analysis, and digital surface mapping directly in Python.

geometry.type: Shapely Polygon or bounding box tuple/list
geometry.description: Spatial region for topographic data extraction (e.g., watershed polygon from PyNHD)

layer.description: Requested topographic layer from 3DEP dynamic service. See documentation for all options.

resolution.description: Target raster resolution. Most US locations support 10m, 30m, or 60m. Dynamic service will resample/output at highest available resolution.

geo_crs.description: CRS of input geometry. Any valid CRS supported; automatic reprojection handled.

crs.type: str or int (limited to CRS:84, EPSG:4326, EPSG:3857, EPSG:5070)
crs.description: Output grid CRS. Limited by service-side options (EPSG:5070 is recommended for projected DEMs).

coords.description: List of coordinate pairs for point elevation querying via elevation_bycoords.

line.description: Line geometry for querying elevation profile at user-provided spacing.

outputs.type: xarray.Dataset, DataArray, CSV, GeoTIFF, or GDAL VRT file (for large requests)
outputs.description: Raster or tabular products: DEMs, slope, aspect, contours as xarray datasets, in-memory arrays, or disk files (NetCDF, GeoTIFF, VRT, CSV); point/query results by coordinate or line.

py3dep.usage_example:
import py3dep
from pynhd import NLDI
geom = NLDI().get_basins('01031500').geometry[0]
dem = py3dep.get_map('DEM', geom, resolution=30, geo_crs=4326, crs=3857)
slope = py3dep.get_map('Slope Degrees', geom, resolution=30)
slope_mpm = py3dep.deg2mpm(slope)
profile = py3dep.elevation_profile(line, spacing=10)
elev = py3dep.elevation_bycoords([[lon, lat], ...], crs=4326)
key_features: Automated querying of USGS 3DEP dynamic and staged services for elevation, slope, aspect, contours, and hillshade products.
Multi-resolution data support (10m, 30m, 60m); dynamic resampling at best available server-side resolution.
Support for numerous layers including DEM, contours (regular & smoothed), slope, aspect (map & degrees), multiple hillshade types, and ellipsoidal height.
Point elevation querying (single/multiple XY pairs), elevation profiles along lines, and adding elevation to xarray objects.
Persistent caching options (configurable via environment variables for low-latency repeated queries).
Efficient parallel data retrieval via async-retriever and PyGeoOGC under the hood.
GDAL VRT output for large dynamic requests and low-memory workflows.
Integration-ready: works with PyGeoHydro, PyNHD, PyGridMET, PyDaymet, and xarray-spatial for end-to-end hydroclimate modeling workflow.
CLI included: batch retrieve DEM/topography to NetCDF or CSV from GeoDataFrame or CSV coordinate input.
Rich example notebooks, installation via pip/conda, and maintained compatibility with the evolving 3DEP web service.
Recommended replacement (for most users): Seamless3DEP, which extends and improves Py3DEP for static and dynamic DEM use (see docs for migration).
notices: Default CRS for get_map is now EPSG:5070 for projected output. EPSG:4326 may return invalid results due to 3DEP service limitations.
Seamless3DEP is the recommended successor package for new workflows.
Request/response caching is highly recommended for performance and reliability.

xrspatial.description: Spatial analysis tools for xarray rasters
xrspatial.purpose: Compute terrain derivatives (slope, aspect, hillshade) from DEM data

outputs.description: Computed terrain derivative

xrspatial.usage_example:
import xrspatial

slope_degrees = xrspatial.slope(dem)
aspect = xrspatial.aspect(dem)
hillshade = xrspatial.hillshade(dem)
key_features: Fast raster analytics
GPU acceleration support
Integration with xarray
Multiple terrain analysis functions

pygeohydro.description: Unified access to major US and global hydroclimate, streamflow, land cover, watershed, soil, flood hazard, water quality, and survey datasets through geospatial web services.
pygeohydro.purpose: Retrieve, query, and analyze NWIS, NID, Water Quality Portal, NLCD land cover, CAMELS attributes, watershed boundary data (WBD), daily ET, irrigation use, Short Term Flood Events, eHydro bathymetry, FEMA flood hazard polygons, and multiple US/global soil datasets.

geometry.description: Spatial input for querying hydrographic features, land cover zones, dam inventory, flood hazards, etc.

ids.description: Feature IDs (NWIS site numbers, NID IDs, HUC codes, CAMELS station, NLCD coordinates)

dates.description: Date ranges for streamflow, daily ET, water quality, survey retrieval, etc.

attributes.description: Dataset-specific fields (e.g. NLCD category, NWIS parameterCd, NID inventory variable)

query.description: Web service filter, SQL clause, or OData string for advanced search (soil, quality, flood hazard, SensorThings)

outputs.description: Hydrology features (points, polygons), time series of streamflow/ET, gridded soil/land cover, statistics and attribute tables

pygeohydro.usage_example:
import pygeohydro as gh
# NWIS daily streamflow
nwis = gh.NWIS()
df = nwis.get_streamflow(['06892350'], ('2010-01-01', '2020-12-31'), mmd=True)
# Land cover for basin geometry
gdf = gh.nlcd_bygeom(basin_geom, years={'cover': [2016, 2019]}, resolution=100)
stats = gh.cover_statistics(gdf['cover_2019'])
rough = gh.overland_roughness(gdf['cover_2016'])
# Water Quality Portal
from pygeohydro import WaterQuality
wq = WaterQuality()
stns = wq.station_bybbox((-92.8, 44.2, -88.9, 46.0), {'characteristicName': 'Caffeine'})
data = wq.data_bystation(stns.MonitoringLocationIdentifier.tolist(), {'characteristicName': 'Caffeine'})
# Dam inventory
from pygeohydro import NID
nid = NID()
dams = nid.get_bygeom((-95, 44, -94, 45), 4326)
key_features: NWIS: USGS streamflow, metadata, station info
CAMELS: Catchment attributes and daily/instantaneous Qobs
NLCD: Land cover, impervious, canopy, cover stats
NID: National Inventory of Dams, by geom, id, attribute
WBD: Hydrologic Unit boundaries, all HUC levels
SSEBop: Actual daily evapotranspiration, by geometry/coords
Water Quality Portal: Chemistry, physical, microbiological data from millions of stations
Irrigation Withdrawals: Monthly HUC12 use (2015)
Short-Term Flood Events: USGS STN event, peaks, high water marks
USACE eHydro: Topobathymetry survey and bathymetry
FEMA NFHL: National Flood Hazard Layer polygons
Soil datasets: gNATSGO, SoilGrids, Derived, POLARIS (30m, global coverage)
SensorThings API: Real-time sensor/query interface
Pre-configured async caching for fast, reliable retrieval
Interactive folium mapping of NWIS stations
Optional plotting: hydrologic signature graphs, NLCD legends
Resilient to service endpoint changes, stable API for modern hydroclimate workflows

pynhd.description: Navigate, query, and analyze US hydrographic and catchment datasets (NHDPlus MR/HR, StreamCat, GeoConnex, EPA, PyGeoAPI, WaterData, mainstems) via web services and vector tools
pynhd.purpose: Access hydrological vector datasets, attributes, and network topologies for basin delineation, flowline extraction, catchment metrics, basin navigation, DEM profiling, and cross-section analysis

geometry.type: shapely.geometry (Point, Polygon, bounding box, MultiPoint, LineString)
geometry.description: Spatial inputs for all queries: points, polygons, bounding boxes for stations, basins, flowlines, and catchments

ids.description: Feature IDs (e.g., USGS station IDs, COMID, HUC12) for lookup and navigation

attributes.description: Attribute names for querying derived datasets (e.g., StreamCat metrics, VAA, ENHD attributes)

sql_cql_clause.description: SQL 92 or CQL query string for advanced attribute or spatial queries

navigation_params.description: Upstream/downstream navigation settings including type ('tributary','mainstem'), source, distance, and direction

outputs.description: Hydrographic features, network graphs, catchment metrics, flowline geometries, attribute tables

pynhd.usage_example:
from pynhd import NLDI, WaterData, PyGeoAPI, network_xsection

nldi = NLDI()
basin = nldi.get_basins('01031500')
flowlines = nldi.navigate_byid(fsource='nwissite', fid='USGS-01031500', navigation='upstreamMain', source='flowlines', distance=1000)
vaa = pynhd.nhdplus_vaa()
flowlines = flowlines.merge(vaa[['comid','slope']], left_on='comid', right_on='comid')
cs = network_xsection(flowlines, distance=4000, width=2000)
key_features: Web service access to NHDPlus MR, HR, StreamCat, GeoConnex, WaterData, EPA catchments
Catchment, flowline, mainstem, gauge, and basin queries via geometry and ID
Network navigation, upstream/downstream tracing, flow accumulation
Cross-section and DEM profile extraction via PyGeoAPI integration
Attribute table merging for millions of segments with metrics like slope, roughness, urbanization, water quality
Networkx graph export for river network analysis
Utilizes persistent caching and async parallel requests for fast big data processing

pynldas2.description: Python API for automated access to NLDAS2 gridded hydroclimate forcing datasets (precipitation, temperature, soil moisture, masks, etc.) across CONUS, supporting queries by coordinate or geometry.
pynldas2.purpose: Retrieve and analyze NLDAS2 hydroclimate variables as gridded or point data from 1979-present. Supports region-based extraction for hydrological modeling and climate analysis.

geometry.description: Defines region for gridded data retrieval (e.g., watershed, catchment, HUC unit)

coords.description: List of coordinates for point data extraction

dates.description: Date range for forcing data time series

variables.description: Hydroclimate variable(s) to download; defaults to all if not specified

source.description: Select data backend. 'grib' (default) or 'netcdf' mirrors original NOAA server sources.

snow.description: If true, splits snow portion of precipitation via temperature thresholds

outputs.description: Time series or gridded hydroclimate fields (e.g. precipitation, temperature, masks) in xarray format

pynldas2.usage_example:
import pynldas2 as nldas
from pygeohydro import WBD
geometry = WBD('huc8').byids('huc8', '13060003').geometry.iloc[0]
clm = nldas.get_bygeom(geometry, '2010-01-08', '2010-01-08', 4326, variables='prcp', snow=True)
mask = nldas.get_grid_mask()
key_features: Direct download of NLDAS2 forcing time series for regions or points
Selection of GRIB or NetCDF sources for redundancy
Automatic separation of snow/rain by temperature threshold
Access to mask layers (land, water, soil, vegetation)
Integration with HyRiver tools for basin geometry and workflow automation
Returns xarray.Dataset objects compatible with hydrological modeling libraries
Pre-configured async caching for fast, large-scale retrieval
Stable API for research hydrology pipelines

hydrosignatures.description: Suite of tools for computing hydrological signatures (indices, regime curves, baseflow extraction, seasonality and flashiness) from streamflow and precipitation time series data.
hydrosignatures.purpose: Analyze streamflow and precipitation records to derive quantitative metrics describing flow characteristics, baseflow contributions, seasonality, flashiness, aridity, and more for hydrological modeling and catchment comparison.

q_mmpt.description: Discharge/streamflow time series in millimeters per unit time (matches precipitation timescale)

p_mmpt.description: Precipitation time series in millimeters per unit time (matches streamflow timescale)

fdc_slope_bins.description: Percentage bins for slope calculation in flow duration curve (default: (33, 67))

bfi_alpha.description: Alpha parameter for baseflow separation filter (Lyne-Hollick method; default: 0.925)

outputs.description: Hydrological signatures as dictionary/json, supports regime curve and signature plots

hydrosignatures.usage_example:
import hydrosignatures as hs
from hydrosignatures import HydroSignatures
sig = HydroSignatures(q_mmpt, p_mmpt)
sigs_dict = sig.values.to_dict()
# Plot monthly regime curve
sig.values.mean_monthly.plot()
# Directly compute baseflow separation
qb = hs.baseflow(q_mmpt, alpha=0.925)
key_features: Compute exceedance probability for flow duration curve plotting
Calculate FDC slope in configurable bins
Flashiness index quantifying hydrograph variability
Mean and rolling monthly summaries for regime curves
Baseflow separation (Lyne-Hollick digital filter)
Baseflow recession and index computations
Aridity and seasonality indices (multiple methods)
Extrema identification for time series
Compare signatures between basins and time periods
HydroSignatures class supports JSON/dictionary serialization and direct plotting
Integration with PyGeoHydro and PyDaymet for data retrieval workflows
Optional numba acceleration and numbagg NaN/fast computation support
Jupyter notebook examples and interactive Binder support

hydrofunctions.description: Access USGS streamflow data from NWIS
hydrofunctions.purpose: Download and process USGS streamflow observations for hydrological analysis

service.description: Service type (dv for daily values, iv for instantaneous)

outputs.description: Streamflow time series with datetime index

hydrofunctions.usage_example:
import hydrofunctions as hf

nwis = hf.NWIS('03335000', 'dv', '2010-01-01', '2020-12-31')
df = nwis.df()
q_cfs = df.iloc[:, 0]
key_features: Simple NWIS data access
Automatic unit handling
Multiple site support
Quality flag parsing

pystac_client.description: Client library for STAC (SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog) APIs
pystac_client.purpose: Search and access cloud-optimized geospatial data from STAC catalogs like Planetary Computer

outputs.description: Collection of STAC items matching search criteria

pystac_client.usage_example:
from pystac_client import Client
import planetary_computer

catalog = Client.open(
    'https://planetarycomputer.microsoft.com/api/stac/v1',
    modifier=planetary_computer.sign_inplace
)
search = catalog.search(
    collections=['modis-15A2H-061'],
    bbox=(-86.9, 40.3, -86.7, 40.5)
)
items = list(search.item_collection())
key_features: Cloud-native geospatial data access
Efficient spatial-temporal search
Integration with major data providers
Support for COG and Zarr formats

planetary_computer.purpose: Sign STAC items and access Microsoft's planetary-scale geospatial data catalog

item_or_collection.description: STAC item to sign for access

outputs.description: Signed STAC item with access credentials

planetary_computer.usage_example:
import planetary_computer

signed_item = planetary_computer.sign(item)
# or use as modifier
catalog = Client.open(
    'https://planetarycomputer.microsoft.com/api/stac/v1',
    modifier=planetary_computer.sign_inplace
)
key_features: Access to petabytes of geospatial data
Automatic credential management
MODIS, Landsat, Sentinel, and more
Cloud-optimized formats

rioxarray.description: Geospatial xarray extension powered by rasterio
rioxarray.purpose: Add rasterio capabilities to xarray for CRS management, clipping, and reprojection

outputs.description: Modified DataArray with spatial operations applied

rioxarray.usage_example:
import rioxarray

# Open raster
da = rioxarray.open_rasterio('data.tif')

# Clip to geometry
clipped = da.rio.clip([geometry], crs='EPSG:4326')

# Reproject
reprojected = da.rio.reproject('EPSG:5070')
key_features: CRS-aware xarray operations
Geometry clipping
Reprojection
Raster I/O with xarray syntax

pygeoglim.description: Access GLiM (Global Lithological Map) and GLHYMPS datasets
pygeoglim.purpose: Extract lithology and hydrogeological properties for watersheds

outputs.description: Dictionary of geological attributes

pygeoglim.usage_example:
from pygeoglim import glim_attributes, glhymps_attributes

# Get lithology
litho = glim_attributes(watershed_gdf)

# Get hydrogeology
hydrogeo = glhymps_attributes(watershed_gdf)
key_features: Global lithology classification
Subsurface permeability
Porosity estimates
Carbonate rock fraction

pysheds.description: Open-source Python library for fast, flexible, and efficient watershed and stream network delineation, flow direction and accumulation, and elevation model conditioning, supporting both D8 and D-infinity routing on DEM grids.
pysheds.purpose: Helps hydrologists, environmental scientists, and GIS analysts process digital elevation data for catchment identification, flow routing, river network extraction, and advanced spatial analysis via raster/vector workflows.

dem_raster.type: GeoTIFF, ASCII grid, or numpy array
dem_raster.description: Digital elevation model (DEM) raster with uniform gridding, ideally conditioned and hydrologically correct.

pour_point.type: tuple/list (x, y coordinates) or cell indices
pour_point.description: Coordinates for catchment outlet (can be snapped to cells with high accumulation).

dirmap.description: Directional mapping for flow direction (e.g., D8: (64,128,1,2,4,8,16,32)), governs routing style.

weights.description: Grid weights for weighted accumulation or flow distance computation.

land_cover_raster.description: Optional raster for combining/seeding hydrologic analysis with land cover data.

soil_shapefile.description: Optional vector polygons for intersecting and rasterizing catchment soil types.

outputs.type: numpy arrays, raster files (GeoTIFF, ASCII), GeoJSON vector, plots
outputs.description: Flow direction grid, accumulation map, catchment mask, river network (GeoJSON), HAND surface, stream order, flow distances, profile/channel indices, and view/crop outputs for visualization.

pysheds.usage_example:
from pysheds.grid import Grid
grid = Grid.from_raster('elevation.tiff')
dem = grid.read_raster('elevation.tiff')
pit_filled_dem = grid.fill_pits(dem)
flooded_dem = grid.fill_depressions(pit_filled_dem)
inflated_dem = grid.resolve_flats(flooded_dem)
fdir = grid.flowdir(inflated_dem, dirmap=(64,128,1,2,4,8,16,32))
acc = grid.accumulation(fdir)
x_snap, y_snap = grid.snap_to_mask(acc > 1000, (x, y))
catch = grid.catchment(x=x_snap, y=y_snap, fdir=fdir, dirmap=(64,128,1,2,4,8,16,32), xytype='coordinate')
branches = grid.extract_river_network(fdir, acc > 50)
dist = grid.distance_to_outlet(x=x_snap, y=y_snap, fdir=fdir)
key_features: Condition DEMs with pit/depression filling and flat resolution for hydrologic correction
Flexible routing: D8 and D-infinity supported for flow direction/accumulation/catchment delineation
Efficient watershed extraction for large grids (multi-million cell performance benchmarks documented)
Catchment delineation via outlet/pour point, with optional snapping to high-flow cells
River network extraction and channel profile generation; stream order computation
Flow accumulation, distance to outlet or ridge, HAND (height above nearest drainage) computation
Integration with land cover rasters and vector soil data; polygonize and rasterize outputs
Convenient I/O: read/write ASCII and GeoTIFF grids, vector overlays with GeoPandas
Visualization and plotting with matplotlib; quick view/crop tools for catchment masking
Open documentation, tutorial media, active releases and Zenodo citation integration
Fast, open-source, cross-platform support; install via pip or conda-forge

benchmarks: river network extraction D8: ~4.07 sec (36M cells)

pysheds.citation: @misc{bartos_2020, title={pysheds: simple and fast watershed delineation in python}, author={Bartos, Matt}, year={2020}, url={https://github.com/mdbartos/pysheds}, doi={10.5281/zenodo.3822494}}

rasterio.description: Python library for reading and writing geospatial raster data
rasterio.purpose: Read DEM, land cover, soil, and other raster datasets

outputs.description: Rasterio dataset object for reading raster data

rasterio.usage_example:
import rasterio

with rasterio.open('dem.tif') as src:
    dem = src.read(1)
    profile = src.profile
    crs = src.crs
key_features: GDAL-based I/O
Cloud-optimized GeoTIFF support
Windowed reading
Affine transformations

geopandas.description: Python library for working with geospatial vector data
geopandas.purpose: Handle watershed boundaries, stream networks, and other vector geometries

filepath_or_geometry.description: Path to vector file or shapely geometry

outputs.description: GeoPandas DataFrame with geometry column

geopandas.usage_example:
import geopandas as gpd

gdf = gpd.read_file('watershed.shp')
area = gdf.geometry.area
gdf_reproj = gdf.to_crs('EPSG:5070')
key_features: Spatial operations
CRS transformations
Geometric manipulations
File I/O for vector formats

xarray.description: Python library for working with labeled multi-dimensional arrays
xarray.purpose: Handle climate data (NetCDF), time series analysis, and spatial-temporal datasets

filepath.description: Path to NetCDF or other array data file

outputs.description: Xarray Dataset with labeled dimensions

xarray.usage_example:
import xarray as xr

ds = xr.open_dataset('climate.nc')
temp = ds['temperature'].sel(time='2020-01-01')
mean_temp = ds['temperature'].mean(dim=['lat', 'lon'])
key_features: Labeled dimensions
NetCDF I/O
Groupby operations
Dask integration for large data

pandas.description: Python library for data manipulation and analysis
pandas.purpose: Handle time series data, streamflow observations, and tabular datasets

outputs.description: Pandas DataFrame for tabular data

pandas.usage_example:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('streamflow.csv', parse_dates=['date'])
df.set_index('date', inplace=True)
monthly = df.resample('M').mean()
key_features: Time series analysis
Data aggregation
Missing data handling
I/O for multiple formats

numpy.description: Fundamental package for numerical computing in Python
numpy.purpose: Array operations, mathematical functions, and numerical algorithms

outputs.description: NumPy array for efficient numerical operations

numpy.usage_example:
import numpy as np

data = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
mean = np.mean(data)
std = np.std(data)
quantile = np.quantile(data, 0.95)
key_features: Fast array operations
Broadcasting
Linear algebra
Random number generation

scipy.purpose: Statistical analysis, optimization, signal processing, and scientific algorithms including curve fitting for seasonality analysis

outputs.description: Depends on specific scipy function used

scipy.usage_example:
from scipy import stats
from scipy.optimize import curve_fit

# Statistical analysis
result = stats.pearsonr(x, y)

# Curve fitting for seasonality
params, _ = curve_fit(sine_function, x, y)
key_features: Statistical tests
Curve fitting and optimization
Signal processing
Interpolation

pyflwdir.description: Fast Python methods for gridded hydro-topography workflows: DEM processing, flow direction analysis, basin/subbasin delineation, upscaling, HAND, stream vector extraction, and more.
pyflwdir.purpose: Earth science, watershed modeling, and hydrological analysis: derive flow directions, delineate catchments, subbasins, stream networks, HAND/floodplain attributes, and upscale hydrography for coarse-grid integration.

dem.description: Digital Elevation Model (DEM) as input for all terrain and hydrology analysis.

flowdir.description: Flow direction raster; can be derived from DEM.

mask.description: Boolean or integer mask for focused analysis and region processing.

parameters.description: Optional algorithm settings (stream order type, px cellsize, Pfafstetter depth, upscaling method, etc.)

outputs.description: Spatial data products: basin/subbasin maps, stream order/classification, HAND, flow paths, upscaled river networks, region summaries, catchment attributes.

pyflwdir.usage_example:
import pyflwdir

# Derive flow direction from DEM
d8 = pyflwdir.dem.fill_depressions(dem)
flowdir = pyflwdir.FlwdirRaster.from_dem(dem)

# Analyze flow direction raster
flw = pyflwdir.FlwdirRaster(flowdir, dem.shape, ftype='d8')
basins = flw.basins()
streams = flw.stream_order('strahler')
hand = flw.hand(drain_mask, dem)
upscaled = flw.upscale(scale_factor=5)

FlwdirRaster.description: Main class for flow direction analysis on 2D raster arrays.
methods: from_dem: derive flow directions from DEM using steepest gradient.
basins: delineate unique basins/subbasins.
stream_order: Strahler or classic stream order mapping.
hand: Height Above Nearest Drainage calculation.
floodplain: delineate floodplain using HAND and area scaling.
upscale: iterative hydrography upscaling for coarse grids.
flowpaths: extract vector flow paths.
accumulate: upstream/downstream accumulation.
pfafstetter: hierarchical Pfafstetter subbasin assignment.
subgrid_rivlen/slope/value: calculate subgrid river length/slope/value at outlets.
hydro_adjusted_elev: hydrologic adjustment of DEM for flow consistency.
stream_vectorization: convert stream raster to vector linestrings.
conversion_utils: switch between D8 <-> LDD, etc.
fill_depressions: fill DEM depressions algorithmically.
slope: calculate DEM gradients.
spread2d: fill array with nearest observations and friction distances.
get_edge: edge extraction for valid cells.
reggrid_area: cell area calculation for grids.
region_bounds: bounding boxes by region label.
region_slices: slice indices for each label.
region_sum: aggregate data by region label.
region_area: compute area by region label.
region_dissolve: merge regions by nearest neighbor.

conversion.description: Methods for conversion between flow direction conventions.
methods: d8_to_ldd: convert D8 array to LDD array.
ldd_to_d8: convert LDD array to D8 array.

key_features: Numba-accelerated for large-scale raster hydrology
DEM to flow direction conversion and repair
Full stream network vectorization and flow paths
Advanced basin and subbasin delineation (Pfafstetter, Strahler, classic)
Floodplain and HAND analysis
Multi-convention flow direction and conversion tools
Upscaling hydrography for coarse models
Automated region metrics and area calculations
Integration with rasterio for I/O
Output in raster and vector formats
